content.problemStatement:
For a permutation PPP and a integer KKK, we define f(P,K)f(P, K)f(P,K) as the set SSS formed by the following process:


Initially, let SSS be an empty set.
For each i=1,2,тАж,Ni = 1, 2, \ldots, Ni=1,2,тАж,N:

Check if there exists xтИИSx \in SxтИИS such that тИгxтИТPiтИгтЙдK|x - P_i| \le KтИгxтИТPiтАЛтИгтЙдK.
Append PiP_iPiтАЛ to SSS if and only if there does not exist such xxx.


You are given a set SSS of elements, represented by a binary array BBB of size NNN. Here, xтИИSx \in SxтИИS if and only if Bx=1B_x = 1BxтАЛ=1.

Does there exist a permutation PPP such that f(P,K)=Sf(P, K) = Sf(P,K)=S. Print Yes\text{Yes}Yes or No\text{No}No accordingly.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains 222 integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN integers - B1,B2,тАж,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1тАЛ,B2тАЛ,тАж,BNтАЛ.




Output Format
For each test case, output Yes\text{Yes}Yes if there exists a valid permutation PPP and No\text{No}No otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : For the permutation P=[1,3,2]P = [1, 3, 2]P=[1,3,2], the set formed is {1,3}\{1, 3\}{1,3} as required.
Test Case 2 : There is no valid permutation PPP.
Test Case 3 : For the permutation P=[2,3,1]P = [2, 3, 1]P=[2,3,1], the set formed is {2}\{2\}{2} as required.
